title: Why Masking Is Essential
concept: Without masking, the RNN will process pad tokens as real inputs, corrupting the hidden state and wasting gradient updates on meaningless slots.

exercises.prompt: What happens if an RNN processes pad tokens without masking?

options.text: It processes them as real inputs, messing up its memory.
It automatically deletes them before calculation.
It runs faster because pad tokens are skipped.

exercises.explain: Pad tokens are artificial placeholders. Without a mask, the model treats them as real data, which corrupts its internal memory (hidden state).

cards.title: The Hidden Threat of Padding
cards.body: We use padding to make all sentences the same length, but how do we stop our AI from learning from this useless filler? Without a special shield, the AI gets confused by its own padding!

cards.title: What is Masking?
cards.body: Masking is a technique that tells our RNN to ignore the 'pad' tokens we added to make sequences the same length. It prevents the model from updating its memory with empty filler data.

cards.title: The Blank Page Skip
cards.body: Imagine reading a book where some pages are blank filler. If you read them out loud, you waste your breath and get confused. Masking is like using sticky notes to cover up those blank sections so your eyes skip straight to the next real chapter!

cards.title: Masking in Action
cards.body: If we process the sentence "Hi [PAD] [PAD]", a masked RNN only updates its hidden state on "Hi". It ignores the two "[PAD]" tokens entirely, keeping its final memory clean and focused only on the actual words.

cards.title: Myth: The AI Knows It's Filler
cards.body: You might think the RNN will naturally learn to ignore '[PAD]' tokens because they contain no information. In reality, the RNN treats them as real inputs, which slowly messes up its hidden state and ruins its learning.

cards.title: Mask vs. No Mask
cards.body: Let's compare how an RNN handles padded sequences with and without masking.

left.text: Processes pads. Hidden state gets corrupted by empty filler.

right.text: Skips pads. Hidden state stays clean and focused on real words.

exercises.prompt: Why is masking essential when training an RNN with padded sequences?

options.text: It increases the size of the vocabulary.
It stops pad tokens from corrupting the hidden state and wasting gradients.
It converts numbers back into text.

exercises.explain: Without a mask, the RNN treats the pad tokens as actual data, which changes the hidden state and wastes time calculating gradients for empty slots.

exercises.prompt: True or False: If you don't use masking, the RNN will still ignore the pad tokens automatically.
exercises.explain: Masking is crucial because it tells the RNN to ignore pad tokens, preventing them from affecting the model's weights and hidden states.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing term to complete the sentence about the danger of unmasked padding.
exercises.sentence: Without masking, pad tokens will corrupt the model's hidden state during training.
exercises.explain: Without masking, the dummy pad values alter the model's hidden state, making it forget the real meaning of the sentence.

exercises.prompt: Find the statement that describes an AI model training WITHOUT masking:

options.text: The AI skips all pad tokens and only calculates gradients for real words.
The AI processes the pad tokens, updating its internal memory state with useless filler.
The AI automatically shrinks the input matrix to remove all empty spaces.

exercises.explain: AI chatbots or models that don't use masking will look at pad tokens as if they are meaningful words, which corrupts their memory of the sentence. Option B correctly identifies this behavior.

exercises.prompt: What happens if you train an RNN on padded sequences without enabling a mask?

options.text: The model automatically deletes pad tokens from the dataset.
The model pauses training and waits for longer sequences to arrive.
The model updates its hidden state using the empty pads, corrupting its memory.
The model gets faster because pad tokens are easier to calculate.

exercises.explain: Without masking, the padded elements are treated as active inputs. The RNN will run its calculations on them, changing the hidden state and generating loss and gradients for useless padding, which wastes training energy and ruins the model's memory.
